# AutoVetting

> A four-step used-car research tool: pinpoint a reliable model, track listings, decode a VIN, get a model-specific pre-purchase inspection checklist.

AutoVetting is a free, unbiased used-car research platform built for buyers who want real mechanical due diligence before handing over money. There are no affiliate links, no sponsored listings, and no dealer partnerships. Data is sourced from the NHTSA vPIC API (vehicle identification and specs), the NHTSA recall database, Technical Service Bulletins (TSBs), aggregated owner-reported failure patterns, and a curated mechanic knowledge base developed from real-world shop experience. The platform is designed for private-party and dealer used-car buyers who want to know a vehicle's common failure points, open recalls, and what to inspect before purchase — independent of who is selling it.

## Tools

- [Pinpoint](https://autovetting.com/pinpoint/): Filter used vehicles by use case, must-have features, seating, and budget to find models with strong reliability track records. Strict-AND filtering ensures every result matches all selected criteria.
- [Search](https://autovetting.com/search/): Track live used-car listings across sources. Filter by make, model, year, price, and mileage.
- [Decode](https://autovetting.com/decode/): Enter a VIN to decode vehicle specs, check for open NHTSA recalls, and surface known TSBs for that exact vehicle configuration.
- [Inspect](https://autovetting.com/inspect/): Get a model-specific pre-purchase inspection checklist tailored to the known failure points of a chosen make/model/year. Designed to be used at the car or sent to a mechanic.

## Repair Guides

- [Repair Guides Index](https://autovetting.com/repair/): Overview of all DIY repair guides with tools, parts lists, torque specs, and step-by-step walkthroughs.
- [Oil Change](https://autovetting.com/repair/oil-change/): Drain plug removal, filter swap, and refill. 30–45 min, $40–$80 in parts. Includes torque specs and common mistakes.
- [Brake Pads + Rotors](https://autovetting.com/repair/brake-pads-rotors/): Full front or rear brake job with caliper removal, rotor swap, and pad bedding. 1.5–3 hours, $80–$150 in parts.
- [Battery Replacement](https://autovetting.com/repair/battery-replacement/): Safe battery swap with correct terminal order, hold-down reinstall, and reset notes. 15–30 min.
- [Cabin Air Filter](https://autovetting.com/repair/cabin-filter/): Behind-the-glovebox filter swap. 15–30 min, no tools required on most vehicles. Improves HVAC airflow and air quality.
- [Engine Air Filter](https://autovetting.com/repair/air-filter/): Airbox access and filter replacement. 10–20 min, no tools on most vehicles. Improves fuel economy and engine protection.






## Blog

Source-backed buyer's guides for specific used vehicles. Each guide covers known issues, NHTSA recalls to verify by VIN, best and worst configurations, market pricing, and inspection priorities.

- [Buying a 2016 Mazda MX-5 Miata (ND): the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2016-mazda-mx5-miata-buyers-guide/): A used 2016-2020 Mazda MX-5 Miata (ND) buyer's guide: the airbag recall on nearly every car, ND1 vs ND2 engines, soft-top leaks, and the safest year to buy.
- [Buying a 2019–2023 Nissan Altima: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2019-nissan-altima-buyers-guide/): Everything you need to know before buying a used 6th-generation Nissan Altima (2019–2023, L34): why the 2.5L PR25DD is the smart pick and the 2.0L KR20DDET VC-Turbo is the one to scrutinize, the June…
- [Buying a 2016–2022 Toyota Prius: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2016-toyota-prius-buyers-guide/): Everything you need to know before buying a used 4th-generation Toyota Prius (2016–2022, ZVW50): the Gen-4 EGR carbon and head-gasket risk, catalytic-converter theft, the J0T engine-harness fire…
- [Buying a 2017–2022 Chrysler Pacifica: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2017-chrysler-pacifica-buyers-guide/): Everything you need to know before buying a used Chrysler Pacifica (2017–2022, RU generation): how the gas V6 and plug-in Hybrid differ and how to decode them, the 948TE 9-speed shift and stall…
- [Buying a 2019 Ram 1500 Classic (DS): the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2019-ram-1500-classic-buyers-guide/): Everything you need to know before buying a 2019 Ram 1500 Classic (DS platform): how it differs from the redesigned DT, the two-engine lineup and how to decode it, the 5.7L Hemi MDS lifter risk, the…
- [Buying a 2021–2023 Ford F-150 (14th gen): the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2021-ford-f150-buyers-guide/): Everything you need to know before buying a 14th-generation Ford F-150 (2021–2023): the five-engine lineup and how to decode it, the PowerBoost hybrid, cam-phaser and 10-speed checks, early-build…
- [Buying a 2018–2022 Honda Accord: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2018-honda-accord-buyers-guide/): Everything you need to know before buying a 10th-gen Honda Accord (2018–2022): the 1.5T vs 2.0T engine split, the cold-climate oil dilution issue, best years, what to pay, and inspection priorities.
- [Buying a 2016–2022 Toyota Tacoma: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2016-toyota-tacoma-buyers-guide/): Everything you need to know before buying a 3rd-gen Toyota Tacoma (2016–2022): known issues, best years, which engine to pick, what to pay, and inspection priorities.
- [Buying a 2021 Toyota Corolla: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2021-toyota-corolla-buyers-guide/): 
- [Buying a 2018 Toyota Camry: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2018-toyota-camry-buyers-guide/): A direct, data-backed guide to buying a 2018 Toyota Camry (XV70). Known issues, best trims, what to pay, and inspection priorities — before you make an offer.
- [Buying a 2017 Honda Civic: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2017-honda-civic-buyers-guide/): 
- [Buying a 2014 Toyota Corolla: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2014-toyota-corolla-buyers-guide/): Everything a used-car buyer needs to know about the 2014–2019 Toyota Corolla (E170): known issues, best and avoid years, what to pay, and inspection priorities.
- [Buying a 2010–2012 Lexus RX 350: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2010-lexus-rx350-buyers-guide/): The 2010–2012 Lexus RX 350 hides one catastrophic flaw behind its luxury badge — and most buyers never check for it. Here's the complete honest guide to buying one safely.
- [Buying a 2014 Honda Accord: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2014-honda-accord-buyers-guide/): 
- [Buying a 2014 Chevrolet Silverado 1500: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2014-chevrolet-silverado-buyers-guide/): A direct, data-backed guide to buying a 2014 Chevrolet Silverado 1500 (K2XX). AFM lifter failures, engine code splits, what to pay, and inspection priorities — before you make an offer.
- [Buying a 2014 Acura TSX: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2014-acura-tsx-buyers-guide/): The 2014 Acura TSX is the swan-song year of one of the most reliable used cars under $10,000. Here is what to check before you buy — K24Z3 VTC rattle, HID ballast failures, Takata recall, and…
- [Buying a 2018 Ford F-150: the honest guide](https://autovetting.com/blog/2018-ford-f150-buyers-guide/): 

## Data Sources

- **NHTSA vPIC API**: Vehicle identification, make/model/year/trim data used throughout Decode and Inspect.
- **NHTSA Recall Database**: Open and historical recall records surfaced during VIN decoding.
- **Technical Service Bulletins (TSBs)**: Manufacturer-issued repair guidance for known issues, integrated into Inspect checklists.
- **Owner-Reported Failure Patterns**: Aggregated community failure data used to weight Pinpoint reliability scores and Inspect checklist priorities.
- **Mechanic Knowledge Base**: Curated shop-floor knowledge from professional mechanics informing repair guides and inspection checklists.

## Optional

The following pages are interactive tools or store personal user data. They are not useful for AI summarization or training and may be skipped:

- [Garage](https://autovetting.com/garage/): Personal vehicle tracker. Stores user-specific saved vehicles. Dynamic, login-gated, no static content of general value.
- [Search](https://autovetting.com/search/): Live listing aggregator. Results are dynamic and ephemeral; no static content worth indexing.
